reasoning: No perfect March Madness brackets survived the first weekend of the 2025 tournament, as extensively tracked and reported by multiple outlets. As per the rules of the X/Uber Eats bracket challenge, the prize for an imperfect but best bracket is $100,000, not the trip to Mars, and the resolution criteria explicitly state that winning the $100,000 without a perfect bracket won't count. Given that achieving a perfect bracket has never been verified in history and all brackets this year have visibly busted, it is near impossible for X to truthfully declare that someone won a trip to Mars on the basis of a perfect bracket. Therefore, the chance of this resolving affirmative is at the floor of acceptable probability inputs.
